title: Index (Chỉ mục)
summary: Bộ slide nguồn Tuần 9 về index, cấu trúc B+Tree, EXPLAIN và tối ưu hiệu suất query.
content_html:
Slide Tuần 9: Index (Chỉ mục)

File nguồn: w09-Indexes.pptx · Loại: Slide PPTX · Số trang hình ảnh: 31 · Trang giới thiệu đã lọc: 2

Slide 1
WHERE vs HAVING
1
SELECT Code, Name, COUNT(Language) AS LangCountFROM country cJOIN countrylanguage cl
ON c.Code = cl.CountryCodeWHERE cl.IsOfficial = "T"GROUP BY Code
Does this work?

Slide 2
WHERE vs HAVING
2
SELECT Code, Name, COUNT(*) AS LangCountFROM country cJOIN countrylanguage cl
ON c.Code = cl.CountryCodeGROUP BY Code
WHERE cl.IsOfficial = "T"
Does this work?

Slide 3
WHERE vs HAVING
3
SELECT Code, Name, COUNT(*) AS LangCountFROM country cJOIN countrylanguage cl
ON c.Code = cl.CountryCodeWHERE cl.IsOfficial = "T“
HAVING cl.IsOfficial = "T"GROUP BY Code
Does this work?

Slide 4
WHERE vs HAVING
4
SELECT Code, Name, COUNT(*) AS LangCountFROM country cJOIN countrylanguage cl
ON c.Code = cl.CountryCodeGROUP BY Code
HAVING cl.IsOfficial = "T"
Does this work?

Slide 5
WHERE vs HAVING
5
SELECT Code, Name, COUNT(*) AS LangCountFROM country cJOIN countrylanguage cl
ON c.Code = cl.CountryCodeGROUP BY Code
WHERE LangCount > 2
Does this work?

Slide 6
WHERE vs HAVING
6
SELECT Code, Name, COUNT(*) AS LangCountFROM country cJOIN countrylanguage cl
ON c.Code = cl.CountryCodeWHERE LangCount > 2
GROUP BY Code
Does this work?

9

Slide 10
(A) What & Why indexes
10

Slide 11
What is index?
11
Index is a data structure that helps (esp. in large data set)
rapidly look up data (WHERE clause)
enforce constraints
sort data (ORDER BY clause)
Costs associated with index:
performance cost: needs updating when data is updated (insert, update, drop)
storage cost
should only be used with selected columns
Common index design: B-Tree (Binary tree)
Indexes are dropped when the associated table is

Slide 12
Binary Tree (B-Tree)
12
Find 145?

Slide 13
When to use index
13
Primary key
Columns used for ORDER BY
Columns used in WHERE clauses:
col = value
col > value

Slide 14
(B) Creating an index
14

Slide 15
Creating an index
15
Add to the CREATE TABLE statement
can be UNIQUE or Non-UNIQUE
CREATE TABLE test (
id INT UNSIGNED NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
string1 VARCHAR(128),
string2 VARCHAR(128),
INDEX str1(string1)
);
implicit index on PK (also unique)
explicit index on column (non-unique)

Slide 16
16
SHOW INDEX FROM test;
index name
unique-ness
data structure

Slide 17
17
CREATE TABLE test (
id INT UNSIGNED NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
string1 VARCHAR(128) UNIQUE,
string2 VARCHAR(128),
UNIQUE INDEX str1(string1)
);
SHOW INDEX FROM test;
UNIQUE indexes on column
UNIQUE indexes on column

Slide 18
Creating an index (2)
18
Use CREATE INDEX statement (after table creation)
can be UNIQUE or Non-UNIQUE
CREATE INDEX str2 ON test(string2);
OR
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX str2 ON test(string2);
SHOW INDEX FROM test;

Slide 19
(C) Showing indexes
19

Slide 20
Showing indexes (1): in one table
20
Use SHOW INDEX FROM statement
Variations:
SHOW INDEXES FROM…
SHOW INDEXES IN …

Slide 21
Showing indexes (1): in all tables in a schema
21
SELECT DISTINCT table_name, index_name
FROM information_schema.statistics
WHERE table_schema = 'scratch';

Slide 22
(D) Dropping indexes
22

Slide 23
Dropping indexes
23
DROP INDEX str1 ON test;
SHOW INDEX FROM test;

Slide 24
(D) Multicolumn indexes
24

Slide 25
Multicolumn indexes
25
Index on more than one columns
CREATE TABLE test (
id INT UNSIGNED NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
string1 VARCHAR(128),
string2 VARCHAR(128),
INDEX twostrs (string1,string2)
);
can also add UNIQUE

Slide 26
Multicolumn indexes
26
SHOW INDEX FROM test;

Slide 27
Multicolumn indexes: explanation
27
EXPLAIN SELECT string1, string2
FROM test
ORDER BY string1, string2;

Slide 28
Single column index: explanation
28
EXPLAIN SELECT string1
FROM test
ORDER BY string1;

Slide 29
Experiment: performance impact of index
29
Drop table test
Recreate it with the 2 column index
Insert 45-50 records into the table
Case 1: query with index (record exec. time)
use the 2-column SELECT query with ORDER BY on both columns (see a previous slide)
Case 2: query without index
drop the index
execute the same query & record the exec. time
Observe the execution time improvement.
